tests.input: CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST: Findings: 2.5 cm mass in right upper lobe. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy. Small right pleural effusion. Impression: Lung mass, concerning for malignancy.

findings: 2.5 cm mass in right upper lobe

tests.input: MRI BRAIN WITHOUT CONTRAST: Findings: 3cm ring-enhancing lesion in the right frontal lobe with surrounding edema. Impression: Brain tumor, recommend biopsy.

findings: 3cm ring-enhancing lesion in right frontal lobe

tests.input: CHEST X-RAY: Findings: Lungs are clear. No pneumothorax or pleural effusion. Cardiac silhouette normal. Impression: Normal chest radiograph.
CT ABDOMEN: Incidental 1.2 cm renal cyst left kidney. 5mm gallbladder polyp. No acute findings. Impression: Benign-appearing renal cyst. Gallbladder polyp, recommend ultrasound follow-up.

EXAM: CT Chest PE Protocol
FINDINGS:
- Pulmonary arteries: No filling defects
- Lungs: No consolidation, no pleural effusion
- Heart: Normal size, no pericardial effusion
- Other: Mediastinum without lymphadenopathy
IMPRESSION:
1. No evidence of pulmonary embolism
CT ABDOMEN: 3.2 x 2.8 cm mass in pancreatic body. Liver lesions: largest 1.5 cm. Impression: Pancreatic mass with liver metastases.
CHEST CT: New 8mm nodule in right lower lobe not present on prior CT from 6 months ago. Previously seen nodules stable. Impression: New pulmonary nodule, recommend short-term follow-up.
CT HEAD: Large acute subdural hematoma with 8mm midline shift. Impression: Acute subdural hematoma, neurosurgery consultation recommended.
